name: Privacy Act 1988 (as amended 2024)
summary: Australia's primary federal privacy legislation, substantially amended in December 2024. It establishes 13 Australian Privacy Principles (APPs) governing how organizations handle personal information, with a statutory tort for serious invasions of privacy and dramatically increased penalties.
keyRequirements: Comply with 13 Australian Privacy Principles (APPs) covering collection, use, disclosure, and storage
Mandatory data breach notification to the OAIC and affected individuals for eligible breaches
Publish a clear and up-to-date privacy policy
Provide individuals with access to and correction of their personal information
Take reasonable steps to protect personal information from misuse, loss, and unauthorized access
Only collect personal information that is reasonably necessary for organizational functions
penalties: Maximum penalty for serious or repeated breaches: the greater of AUD 50 million, three times the benefit obtained, or 30% of adjusted turnover. Mid-tier penalties of AUD 3.3 million for bodies corporate. Enforced by the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC).
